notes: Assertion 'ADK section names specific callback types' passes in 5/6 runs -- callbacks are mentioned by both configs, but only the skill produces dedicated structured sections. Partially non-discriminating.
Eval 2 with_skill failed Prior Art URL assertion: used citation-style refs (arXiv 2308.03688) without full https:// URLs. The section existed and was substantive.
Baselines used more tokens (102k avg vs 91k) while achieving much lower structural pass rates (19% vs 95%) -- deeper codebase exploration but without the multi-angle framework.

Eval 1 baseline (346s, 118k tokens) found a critical issue (plugin doesn't fire for child dispatches) that the with-skill report missed -- baselines can find codebase-specific bugs the skill's structured approach may overlook.
Eval 3 (inline cron plan) shows strongest differentiation: with-skill found external prior art (VIGIL, PALADIN, Temporal.io) while baseline focused entirely on codebase-internal analysis.
The skill's value is the structured 3-critic framework (ADK callbacks, vision alignment, prior art) which baselines never spontaneously produce. Quality of individual critiques is comparable.
